exploration_log.aha_moment: The padded_string_builder::operator= at line 292 already has `if (this != &o)` showing the desired pattern; I just needed to add the same guard to padded_string::operator= at line 99.

key_decisions.decision: Add a simple `if (this == &o) { return *this; }` guard at the top of the operator
key_decisions.reasoning: Task description explicitly prescribes this exact guard wording; it's also the simplest and most idiomatic fix

queries.query_text: Fix padded_string move assignment operator in padded_string-inl.h to guard against self-assignment before deleting data_ptr

solve_notes: Added a single-line self-assignment guard `if (this == &o) { return *this; }` at the top of `padded_string::operator=(padded_string&&)` in `padded_string-inl.h`, preventing the use-after-free when `x = std::move(x)` is called.
task_text: The `padded_string` move assignment operator in `padded_string-inl.h` does not check for self-assignment before transferring ownership. When called as `x = std::move(x)`, the operator first executes `delete[] data_ptr`, freeing the buffer, and then copies the now-freed `data_ptr` and stale `viable_size` from the source object (which is the same object), resulting in a use-after-free. Add a self-assignment guard `if (this == &o) { return *this; }` at the top of `padded_string::operator=(padded_string&&)` in `padded_string-inl.h`.
